format: A connected text with gaps. To the right of each line is a CAPITALISED key word — put it into the correct grammatical form: verb tense and voice, non-finite forms, comparative/superlative of adjectives and adverbs, plural nouns, pronouns, numerals.
scoring: Tasks 19–24, 1 point each. Spelling counts — a correct form spelt wrong gets 0.
strategy: Read the whole sentence (and the neighbouring ones) first — the form depends on tense and meaning.
Decide the part of speech and what's needed: verb → tense/voice; adjective → comparison; noun → plural; pronoun → case.
Watch time markers (yesterday, already, since) and passive signals (by, is/are done).
Don't get stuck — mark a hard gap, move on and come back.
Write the FULL form and mind the spelling (doubled letters, -ied, irregular verbs).
common_mistakes: Wrong tense — ignoring time markers or the sequence of tenses.
Forgetting the plural or the third-person -s.
time: 6–8 minutes for both grammar/vocabulary blocks. Don't linger on one gap.

format: Two parts. Word formation (25–29): build a related word from the CAPITALISED key word using prefixes/suffixes (including negatives un-, in-, im-, dis-). Vocabulary (30–36): choose one of four words that fits the collocation and meaning.
scoring: 1 point per gap. Spelling matters in word formation.
strategy: Word formation: first decide the part of speech needed (noun/adjective/adverb/verb), then the right affix.
Check the meaning — if it's negative, add un-/in-/im-/dis-.
Vocabulary: choose by collocation (which word goes with the others), not by guessing.
Re-read the sentence with your word — does it sound natural and fit the grammar?
common_mistakes: Right root but wrong part of speech.
Spelling errors in the new word.
Vocabulary: picking a synonym that doesn't collocate.
time: 6–8 minutes. Do word formation thoughtfully — that's where mistakes hide.

format: Three formats. Headings: match texts to headings (one heading is extra). Gapped text: insert sentence-parts into the gaps (one part is extra) — it tests coherence. Multiple choice: read a long text and choose the best answer for each question.
strategy: Headings: read each text's main idea and match the gist — don't get hooked by a repeated word.
Gapped text: use the links around the gap (pronouns, this/such, connectors) to find the right part.
Multiple choice: find the place in the text that answers the question — the answer is paraphrased, not copied.
Eliminate options that say more or less than the text actually does.
common_mistakes: Matching by a single repeated word instead of by meaning.
Forgetting the extra heading / extra part.
Multiple choice: picking an option that's true in general but not stated in the text.
time: About 30 minutes. Headings fast (5–7 min); spend the rest on the gapped text and multiple choice.

format: You get an extract from a pen-friend's email on a topic. Write a reply: answer ALL their questions AND ask 3 questions of your own on the given subject. About 100 words, informal style.
scoring: Max 6 points: K1 task (all answers + your 3 questions + style), K2 organisation, K3 language. Under 90 words → heavy penalty.
strategy: Find every question in the extract and answer each one fully.
Ask 3 real questions on the stated subject — don't forget them, it's a big part of the score.
Keep an informal style: contractions, simple linkers, an opening, a friendly closing and your name.
Use paragraphs and linking words — that's a separate criterion.
Count the words; aim for ~100 (a safe range).
common_mistakes: Missing one of the friend's questions.
Formal style instead of a friendly one.
time: ~20 minutes: 2–3 min plan, 12–13 min writing, 4–5 min check.

task: Opinion text based on a chart (task 38)
format: A written project: you get a project topic and a table/chart. Write a structured text with 6 aspects: an intro to the topic; 2–3 facts from the data; 1–2 problems and a solution; your opinion with reasons; a conclusion. About 200–250 words, neutral style.
scoring: Max 14 points across 5 criteria (K1–K5). All 6 aspects must be present; no copying chunks from the prompt.
strategy: Plan all 6 aspects before writing — a missing aspect costs a lot.
Describe 2–3 real numbers from the chart in your own words.
State a problem AND a solution; give your opinion WITH reasons.
Use formal linkers and one paragraph per aspect.
Don't copy phrases from the prompt — paraphrase them.
Check the length (~200–250) and that every aspect is there.
common_mistakes: Copying text straight from the prompt.
No data from the chart, only general talk.
Wrong length or an informal style.
time: 30–35 minutes: 5 min plan, 20–22 min writing, 5–7 min check.
